Chapter IV.—God desires not sacrifices, but righteousness.

ccel.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

title: Chapter IV.—God desires not sacrifices, but righteousness.

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

verses.book: On the Sole Government of God
verses.text: And that God is not appeased by the libations and incense of evil-doers, but awards vengeance in righteousness to each one, Philemon again shall bear testimony to me:—

verses.book: On the Sole Government of God
verses.text: Again, Plato, in Timæus, says: “But if any one on consideration should actually institute a rigid inquiry, he would be ignorant of the distinction between the human and the divine nature; because God mingles many things up into one, [and again is able to dissolve one into many things,] seeing that He is endued with knowledge and power; but no man either is, or ever shall be, able to perform any of these.”
